summary.diff_scope_note: All four files are infrastructure artifacts (bash hook, python hook, JSON config, pytest tests). The verbose section patterns (## Philosophy, ## Overview, ## Quality Standards, ## Safety Features, ## Helper Scripts, ## Usage) are absent from all files. Hook header comments in pretool-gitignore-preflight.py (lines 2-27, 26 lines) and userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh (lines 2-26, 25 lines, pre-existing) are technical contract documentation specifying input shapes, no-op conditions, blocking conditions, and behavioral contracts — these are rules, not stories. The 8 lines newly added to userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh in this diff include 3 inline 'why' comments referencing arch-3 spec R5a and 5 lines of functional guard logic. settings.json adds 4 lines of JSON config. The test file (417 lines) is a pytest test suite with AC-labeled section separators and function docstrings — standard pytest convention, no verbose documentation sections.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed NONE finding is correct. All three feedback items were OBSERVATION_ONLY with no PROPOSED_FIX (no blockers). Key confirmations: (1) these files are hooks/config/tests, not command/agent documentation; (2) critical verbosity patterns are absent; (3) comments in the hook files are contract/why metadata, not 'rules not stories' verbosity. One precision observation logged: pretool-gitignore-preflight.py header spans lines 2-27 (26 lines, not 28/29) — no impact on severity math since threshold is not approached.

findings.issue: No blockers found. The draft NONE finding is correct: these files are hooks/config/tests, not command/agent documentation, and the critical verbosity patterns are absent.
findings.rationale: Confirmation with no proposed change required.

findings.issue: Comments in pretool-gitignore-preflight.py, userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh, and pytest section/docstring text in test_arch6_arch3.py are contract/why/test metadata, not 'rules not stories' verbosity.
Minor precision: preflight hook header spans lines 2-27 (26 lines), so '28/29 line' wording should not be used for severity math.
